title: Kaoru Nagisa and Ode to Joy
display_title: Kaoru Nagisa and Ode to Joy
wikitext:
[]: Kaoru Nagisa and Ode to Joy             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Wed Sep 15, 2004 4:22 pm How is the song Ode to Joy related to Kaoru Nagisa? When we first see him he is humming it out loud, then later when Shinji is waiting for him before they go to the shower together you hear the song again. Lastly when Kaoru takes over Unit 02 and heads towards Lillith the song is played again. What does it mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Wed Sep 15, 2004 4:40 pm The song is about destiny, finality. I don't know how else to put it.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Wed Sep 15, 2004 5:41 pm ~Ill have to agree with hex its about reaching the highest state of inlightentment the peak of your exsistance. I think the reason they used it is because it also to me syboliss brotherhood the under standing of humans as a whole ~If you dont understand i totaly understand becase i dont wholey understand what the hell i just wrote. ~Its kinda scary :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Wed Sep 15, 2004 7:03 pm Kaworu is a J3w. He's the original Fiddler On The Roof.

[]: Re: Kaoru Nagisa and Ode to Joy             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 15, 2004 7:49 pm Gilgamesh wrote: ...then later when Shinji is waiting for him before they go to the shower together you hear the song again.

[]: Re: Kaoru Nagisa and Ode to Joy             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Wed Sep 15, 2004 7:54 pm Dave wrote: Gilgamesh wrote: ...then later when Shinji is waiting for him before they go to the shower together you hear the song again.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 15, 2004 7:57 pm How does it seem strange? Shinji meets Kaworu and hears him humming ode to joy; Shinji likes Kaworu and Kaworu says that he likes Ode to Joy (sort of), so Shinji goes out and gets a tape of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Sep 15, 2004 8:04 pm Dave wrote: How does it seem strange? Shinji meets Kaworu and hears him humming ode to joy; Shinji likes Kaworu and Kaworu says that he likes Ode to Joy (sort of), so Shinji goes out and gets a tape of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Wed Sep 15, 2004 8:36 pm Dave wrote: How does it seem strange? Shinji meets Kaworu and hears him humming ode to joy; Shinji likes Kaworu and Kaworu says that he likes Ode to Joy (sort of), so Shinji goes out and gets a tape of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Wed Sep 15, 2004 9:07 pm im listening to it right now

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 15, 2004 9:47 pm Phaze wrote: Dave wrote: How does it seem strange? Shinji meets Kaworu and hears him humming ode to joy; Shinji likes Kaworu and Kaworu says that he likes Ode to Joy (sort of), so Shinji goes out and gets a tape of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Wed Sep 15, 2004 10:46 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: The song is about destiny, finality. I don't know how else to put it.

[]:             Quote Post by advent_child » Thu Sep 16, 2004 4:25 am Hexon.Arq wrote: The song is about destiny, finality. I don't know how else to put it.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Sep 16, 2004 7:09 am Freedom is the main theme of Beethoven's 9th Symphony, and Kaworu is Tabris, the Angel of Free Will.

[]:             Quote Post by advent_child » Thu Sep 16, 2004 8:13 am Shin-seiki wrote: Freedom is the main theme of Beethoven's 9th Symphony, and Kaworu is Tabris, the Angel of Free Will.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Sep 16, 2004 8:18 am advent_child wrote: Shin-seiki wrote: Freedom is the main theme of Beethoven's 9th Symphony, and Kaworu is Tabris, the Angel of Free Will.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Sep 16, 2004 10:55 am Phaze wrote: Dave wrote: How does it seem strange? Shinji meets Kaworu and hears him humming ode to joy; Shinji likes Kaworu and Kaworu says that he likes Ode to Joy (sort of), so Shinji goes out and gets a tape of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Sep 16, 2004 4:52 pm Ah, wrong scene, I was referring to a certain moment in Death and Rebirth.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Sep 16, 2004 5:25 pm Don't scare me like that Phaze.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Thu Sep 16, 2004 6:51 pm :shock: Geez, you people are dense ! The simple answer is...Kaworu likes milk! I mean, it's obvious! He was humming the "Drink milk, love life" jingle, which only sounds like "Ode to Joy". Kaworu: *sitting on debris near the crater lake* Drink milk, love life, taste the freshness, gives you more life en-ner-gy, drink milk, love life, drink milk, love life, drink milk, love life, driiiiink milk. *Pauses* I think that song is the high point of the Lilim culture, don't you agree? Shinji:... ...no!
